caption: The image displays four sequential frames from an educational presentation on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" for "Tutorial – 1," delivered by "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay." The slides feature a dark blue background with white and yellow text. A mathematical formula for $\gamma$ is presented at the top, alongside the value $\epsilon_4 = 0.85$. Below this is a data table with columns labeled "1", "2", "f", and "g," listing values for pressure "p (bar)", temperature "T (K)", enthalpy "h (j/g)", and entropy "s (j/gK)". A calculation for $\gamma_4$ is shown at the bottom. As the frames progress, the calculation is completed step-by-step: the first frame shows the setup, the second frame adds the results "2.7195" and "400.3", and the final two frames add the final result "= 0.006". The NPTEL logo is visible in the bottom left corner of each frame.
